threshold_count: 9 or more symptoms from any of the five categories (intrusion, mood, dissociation, avoidance, arousal)

symptoms.description: The patient has been directly exposed to, witnessed, or learned about a traumatic event involving actual or threatened death, serious injury, or sexual violence.
The patient experiences recurrent, involuntary, and intrusive distressing memories of the traumatic event.
The patient experiences recurrent distressing dreams in which the content or affect of the dream is related to the traumatic event.
The patient experiences dissociative reactions, such as flashbacks, in which they feel or act as if the traumatic event were recurring.

The patient experiences intense or prolonged psychological distress or marked physiological reactions in response to internal or external cues that symbolize or resemble an aspect of the traumatic event.
The patient experiences a persistent inability to experience positive emotions, such as an inability to feel happiness, satisfaction, or loving feelings.
The patient experiences an altered sense of the reality of their surroundings or themselves, such as seeing themselves from another's perspective, being in a daze, or time slowing.
The patient is unable to remember an important aspect of the traumatic event, typically due to dissociative amnesia rather than other factors like head injury or substances.
The patient makes efforts to avoid distressing memories, thoughts, or feelings about or closely associated with the traumatic event.

The patient makes efforts to avoid external reminders, such as people, places, conversations, activities, objects, or situations, that arouse distressing memories, thoughts, or feelings associated with the traumatic event.
The patient experiences insomnia or other significant sleep disturbances.
The patient exhibits irritable behavior and angry outbursts with little or no provocation, typically expressed as verbal or physical aggression toward people or objects.
The patient exhibits hypervigilance.
The patient experiences problems with concentration.
The patient exhibits an exaggerated startle response.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 6 or more symptoms (5 for adults 17+) of inattention or hyperactivity

symptoms.description: The patient often fails to give close attention to details or makes careless mistakes in schoolwork, at work, or during other activities.
The patient often has difficulty sustaining attention in tasks or play activities.
The patient often does not seem to listen when spoken to directly.
The patient often does not follow through on instructions and fails to finish schoolwork, chores, or duties in the workplace.
The patient often has difficulty organizing tasks and activities.
The patient often avoids, dislikes, or is reluctant to engage in tasks that require sustained mental effort.
The patient often loses things necessary for tasks or activities.
The patient is often easily distracted by extraneous stimuli.
The patient is often forgetful in daily activities.
The patient often fidgets with or taps their hands or feet, or squirms in their seat.
The patient often leaves their seat in situations where remaining seated is expected.
The patient often runs about or climbs in situations where it is inappropriate, or experiences subjective feelings of restlessness if an adolescent or adult.
The patient is often unable to play or engage in leisure activities quietly.
The patient is often "on the go," acting as if they are driven by a motor.
The patient often talks excessively.
The patient often blurts out an answer before a question has been completed.
The patient often has difficulty waiting their turn.
The patient often interrupts or intrudes on others during conversations or activities.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Distress out of proportion to stressor OR significant functional impairment
duration_rule: Begins within 3 months of stressor; ends within 6 months of stressor termination

symptoms.description: The patient develops emotional or behavioral symptoms in response to an identifiable stressor within 3 months of its onset.
The patient develops emotional or behavioral symptoms in response to a stressor that are out of proportion to the severity or intensity of the stressor.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Marked fear or anxiety in at least 2 of 5 specific situations

symptoms.description: The patient experiences marked fear or anxiety about using public transportation, such as automobiles, buses, trains, ships, or planes.
The patient experiences marked fear or anxiety about being in open spaces, such as parking lots, marketplaces, or bridges.
The patient experiences marked fear or anxiety about being in enclosed places, such as shops, theaters, or cinemas.
The patient experiences marked fear or anxiety about standing in line or being in a crowd.
The patient experiences marked fear or anxiety about being outside of the home alone.

The patient fears or avoids situations because of thoughts that escape might be difficult or help might not be available in the event of developing panic-like, incapacitating, or embarrassing symptoms.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 1 or more of 6 physical/neurological signs plus maladaptive behavioral changes

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits clinically significant problematic behavioral or psychological changes, such as inappropriate sexual or aggressive behavior, mood lability, or impaired judgment, during or shortly after alcohol ingestion.
The patient exhibits slurred, unclear speech shortly after alcohol ingestion.
The patient exhibits impaired coordination during or shortly after alcohol ingestion.
The patient exhibits an unsteady gait and poor coordination shortly after alcohol ingestion.
The patient exhibits nystagmus, or jerking eye movements, shortly after alcohol ingestion.
The patient exhibits an impairment in attention or memory shortly after alcohol ingestion.
The patient exhibits stupor or coma during or shortly after alcohol ingestion.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 2 or more of 8 symptoms
duration_rule: Hours to days after stopping heavy, lengthy use

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits autonomic hyperactivity, such as sweating or a rapid pulse, during substance withdrawal.

The patient exhibits an involuntary trembling or shaking, presenting as a symptom of central nervous system overactivity following the abrupt cessation or reduction of heavy, prolonged alcohol or substance consumption.
The patient experiences insomnia or other significant sleep disturbances.
The patient experiences nausea or vomiting as a symptom of substance withdrawal.
The patient experiences transient visual, tactile, or auditory hallucinations or illusions during substance withdrawal.
The patient exhibits psychomotor agitation, such as pacing, hand-wringing, or an inability to sit still.
The patient experiences significant anxiety as a symptom of substance withdrawal.
The patient experiences generalized tonic-clonic seizures as a severe manifestation of alcohol or substance withdrawal.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Restriction of energy intake + intense fear of weight gain + body image distortion

symptoms.description: The patient severely restricts food intake, leading to a markedly reduced body weight that is less than minimally expected for their age and height.
The patient exhibits an intense fear of gaining weight or becoming fat, or persistently behaves in ways that interfere with weight gain.
The patient has a distorted self-perception of being overweight despite actually being severely underweight.

diagnosis_name: Anxiety Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
threshold_count: Physiological cause of prominent panic attacks or marked anxiety
duration_rule: Not specified (physiological consequence of medical condition)

symptoms.description: The patient has a disturbance that is the direct pathophysiological consequence of another medical condition, as evidenced by history, physical examination, or laboratory findings.
The patient experiences recurrent or isolated panic attacks, characterized by sudden surges of intense fear or discomfort accompanied by physical and cognitive symptoms.
The patient experiences prominent and severe anxiety symptoms or panic attacks.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Panic attacks or anxiety predominant in the clinical picture following substance use
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient developed symptoms during or soon after substance intoxication, withdrawal, or medication exposure, and the substance is capable of producing the anxiety symptoms.
The patient experiences recurrent or isolated panic attacks, characterized by sudden surges of intense fear or discomfort accompanied by physical and cognitive symptoms.
The patient experiences prominent and severe anxiety symptoms or panic attacks.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Failure to meet nutrition/energy needs + at least 1 of 4 specified consequences

symptoms.description: The patient persistently avoids or restricts food intake based on a lack of interest, sensory characteristics of food, or concern about aversive consequences of eating.
The patient exhibits significant weight loss or a failure to achieve expected weight gain due to food avoidance.
The patient experiences a significant nutritional deficiency as a result of restrictive food intake.
The patient is dependent on enteral feeding or oral nutritional supplements to meet their nutritional needs.
The patient experiences marked interference with psychosocial functioning as a result of food avoidance.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 3/3 social communication deficits AND at least 2/4 restricted/repetitive behaviors

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits deficits in social-emotional reciprocity, ranging from abnormal social approach and failure of normal conversation to reduced sharing of interests, emotions, or affect.
The patient exhibits deficits in nonverbal communicative behaviors used for social interaction, ranging from poorly integrated communication to abnormalities in eye contact and body language.
The patient exhibits deficits in developing, maintaining, and understanding relationships, ranging from difficulties adjusting behavior to suit social contexts to difficulties in making friends.
The patient engages in stereotyped or repetitive motor movements, use of objects, or speech, such as simple motor stereotypies, echolalia, or idiosyncratic phrases.
The patient insists on sameness, inflexible adherence to routines, or ritualized patterns of verbal or nonverbal behavior, often showing extreme distress at small changes.
The patient exhibits highly restricted, fixated interests that are abnormal in intensity or focus, such as a strong attachment to unusual objects or excessively circumscribed interests.
The patient exhibits hyper- or hypo-reactivity to sensory input or an unusual interest in sensory aspects of the environment, such as indifference to pain or adverse responses to specific sounds.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 3 or more traits since age 15 + evidence of Conduct Disorder before age 15
duration_rule: Pervasive since age 15; patient is at least 18 years old

symptoms.description: The patient fails to conform to social norms regarding lawful behaviors, repeatedly engaging in acts that are grounds for arrest.
The patient exhibits deceitfulness, as indicated by repeated lying, use of aliases, or conning others for personal profit or pleasure.
The patient exhibits impulsivity or a failure to plan ahead.
The patient exhibits irritability and aggressiveness, as indicated by repeated physical fights or assaults.
The patient exhibits a reckless disregard for the safety of themselves or others.
The patient exhibits consistent irresponsibility, as indicated by a repeated failure to sustain consistent work behavior or honor financial obligations.
The patient lacks remorse, showing indifference to or actively rationalizing having hurt, mistreated, or stolen from another person.
The patient has a history of conduct disorder with an onset before age 15 years.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 4 or more specific traits
duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient avoids occupational activities that involve significant interpersonal contact due to fears of criticism, disapproval, or rejection.
The patient is unwilling to get involved with people unless they are certain of being liked.
The patient shows restraint within intimate relationships due to the fear of being shamed or ridiculed.
The patient is overly sensitive to criticism, disapproval, or rejection, leading to profound social inhibition and avoidance of interpersonal contact.
The patient is inhibited in new interpersonal situations because of feelings of inadequacy.
The patient views themselves as socially inept, personally unappealing, or inferior to others.
The patient is unusually reluctant to take personal risks or to engage in any new activities because they may prove embarrassing.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Preoccupation with 1 or more perceived physical flaws plus repetitive behaviors or mental acts

symptoms.description: The patient is preoccupied with one or more perceived defects or flaws in their physical appearance that are unobservable or appear slight to others.
The patient performs repetitive behaviors or mental acts, such as mirror checking or excessive grooming, in response to their appearance concerns.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Binges plus 3 or more behavioral symptoms (e.g., eating fast, eating until uncomfortably full)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences recurrent episodes of binge eating involving large quantities of food.
The patient experiences a sense of loss of control over eating during a binge episode.
The patient eats much more rapidly than normal during a binge eating episode.
The patient eats until feeling uncomfortably full during a binge eating episode.
The patient eats large amounts of food when not feeling physically hungry.
The patient eats alone because of feeling embarrassed by how much they are eating during a binge episode.
The patient feels disgusted with themselves, depressed, or very guilty after an episode of binge eating.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: IQ range of 71–84

symptoms.description: The patient has an IQ in the range of 71 to 84, indicating borderline intellectual functioning.
The patient exhibits an absence of coping problems typically associated with intellectual developmental disorder.

threshold_count: Eating in binges followed by inappropriate compensatory behavior

symptoms.description: The patient recurrently consumes an objectively large amount of food in a discrete period of time, accompanied by a sense of lack of control over eating.
The patient experiences a sense of loss of control over eating during a binge episode.
The patient recurrently engages in inappropriate compensatory behaviors, such as self-induced vomiting, misuse of laxatives, or excessive exercise, to prevent weight gain.
The patient's self-evaluation is unduly influenced by their body shape and weight.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 5 or more specific traits
duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient makes frantic efforts to avoid real or imagined abandonment.
The patient demonstrates a pattern of unstable and intense interpersonal relationships.
The patient exhibits a markedly and persistently unstable self-image or sense of self.
The patient displays impulsivity in at least two areas that are potentially self-damaging.
The patient engages in recurrent suicidal behavior, gestures, or self-mutilating behavior.
The patient experiences affective instability due to a marked reactivity of mood.
The patient experiences chronic feelings of emptiness or boredom.
The patient exhibits inappropriate, intense anger or has difficulty controlling their anger.
The patient experiences transient, stress-related paranoid ideation or severe dissociative symptoms.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least one manic episode (3 or more symptoms; 4 if only irritable)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits a persistent, abnormal increase in goal-directed activity or energy.
The patient experiences inflated self-esteem or grandiosity.
The patient experiences a decreased need for sleep, feeling rested after only a few hours of sleep.
The patient is more talkative than usual or feels a persistent pressure to keep talking.
The patient experiences a flight of ideas or a subjective feeling that their thoughts are racing.
The patient is easily distracted by unimportant or irrelevant external stimuli.
The patient shows a marked increase in goal-directed activity or exhibits psychomotor agitation.
The patient excessively engages in activities that have a high potential for painful consequences, such as buying sprees, sexual indiscretions, or foolish business investments.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least 1 hypomanic episode AND at least 1 major depressive episode

symptoms.description: The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits a persistent, abnormal increase in goal-directed activity or energy.
The patient experiences inflated self-esteem or grandiosity.
The patient experiences a decreased need for sleep, feeling rested after only a few hours of sleep.
The patient is more talkative than usual or feels a persistent pressure to keep talking.
The patient experiences a flight of ideas or a subjective feeling that their thoughts are racing.
The patient is easily distracted by unimportant or irrelevant external stimuli.
The patient shows a marked increase in goal-directed activity or exhibits psychomotor agitation.
The patient excessively engages in activities that have a high potential for painful consequences, such as buying sprees, sexual indiscretions, or foolish business investments.
The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences a markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all, or almost all, activities most of the day.
The patient experiences significant weight loss or gain, or a decrease or increase in appetite nearly every day.
The patient experiences insomnia or hypersomnia nearly every day.
The patient exhibits psychomotor agitation or retardation that is observable by others.
The patient experiences fatigue or a loss of energy nearly every day.
The patient experiences feelings of worthlessness or excessive, inappropriate guilt nearly every day.
The patient experiences a diminished ability to think or concentrate, or indecisiveness, nearly every day.
The patient has recurrent thoughts of death, recurrent suicidal ideation, or makes a suicide attempt.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Bipolar Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
threshold_count: Physiological cause of manic or hypomanic symptoms

symptoms.description: The patient has evidence from their history, physical examination, or laboratory findings that the disturbance is the direct pathophysiological consequence of another medical condition.
The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits a persistent, abnormal increase in goal-directed activity or energy.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Manic or hypomanic symptoms following substance use
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient has evidence from their history, physical examination, or laboratory findings that the symptoms developed during or soon after substance intoxication or withdrawal, or after exposure to a medication capable of producing manic or hypomanic symptoms.
The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits a persistent, abnormal increase in goal-directed activity or energy.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: At least 1 (delusions, hallucinations, disorganized speech, or behavior)
duration_rule: 1 day to 1 month (full return to baseline)

symptoms.description: The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient exhibits speech that is noticeably incoherent, disorganized, or frequently derails from the topic.
The patient exhibits severely abnormal psychomotor behavior, ranging from unresponsiveness to agitated, purposeless movement.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

threshold_count: 5 or more of 12 symptoms following high-dose consumption (typically 250mg+)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences restlessness following caffeine consumption.
The patient experiences nervousness following caffeine consumption.
The patient exhibits excitement following caffeine consumption.
The patient experiences insomnia following caffeine consumption.
The patient exhibits a flushed face following caffeine consumption.
The patient experiences diuresis following caffeine consumption.
The patient experiences gastrointestinal disturbance following caffeine consumption.
The patient experiences muscle twitching shortly after consuming an excessive amount of caffeine.
The patient exhibits a rambling flow of thought and speech shortly after consuming caffeine.
The patient experiences tachycardia or cardiac arrhythmia following caffeine consumption.
The patient experiences periods of inexhaustibility following caffeine consumption.
The patient exhibits psychomotor agitation following caffeine consumption.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 3 or more of 5 symptoms following abrupt cessation of daily use

symptoms.description: The patient experiences headaches as a symptom of withdrawal after abruptly stopping or reducing caffeine intake.
The patient experiences marked fatigue or drowsiness following the abrupt cessation or reduction of caffeine intake.
The patient experiences a depressed mood or irritability following the abrupt cessation or reduction of caffeine intake.
The patient experiences difficulty concentrating following the abrupt cessation or reduction of caffeine intake.
The patient experiences flu-like symptoms following the abrupt cessation or reduction of caffeine intake.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Catatonic Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: At least 3 prominent symptoms of catatonia
duration_rule: Not specified (physiological consequence of medical condition)

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits stupor, characterized by a lack of psychomotor activity and unresponsiveness to the environment.
The patient maintains a rigid, uncomfortable posture against gravity even when told it is not necessary to do so.
The patient exhibits waxy flexibility, actively resisting attempts to change their position before yielding to pressure.
The patient exhibits an absence of, or very little, verbal response despite the evident physical ability to speak.
The patient exhibits motiveless resistance to instructions or attempts to be moved.
The patient voluntarily assumes and maintains an unnatural or uncomfortable pose.
The patient performs repeated movements that seem to have a goal but are excessive for the purpose.
The patient engages in repetitive, abnormally frequent, non-goal-directed movements.
The patient exhibits excessive motor activity that appears to have neither a purpose nor an external cause.
The patient exhibits facial contortions or grimacing not made in response to a noxious stimulus.
The patient exhibits verbatim, automatic repetition of someone else's words.
The patient automatically mimics or imitates another person's physical behavior.

diagnosis_name: Catatonia Associated with Another Mental Disorder (Specifier)
chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: At least 3 out of 12 characteristics

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits stupor, characterized by a lack of psychomotor activity and unresponsiveness to the environment.
The patient maintains a rigid, uncomfortable posture against gravity even when told it is not necessary to do so.
The patient exhibits waxy flexibility, actively resisting attempts to change their position before yielding to pressure.
The patient exhibits an absence of, or very little, verbal response despite the evident physical ability to speak.
The patient exhibits motiveless resistance to instructions or attempts to be moved.
The patient voluntarily assumes and maintains an unnatural or uncomfortable pose.
The patient performs repeated movements that seem to have a goal but are excessive for the purpose.
The patient engages in repetitive, abnormally frequent, non-goal-directed movements.
The patient exhibits excessive motor activity that appears to have neither a purpose nor an external cause.
The patient exhibits facial contortions or grimacing not made in response to a noxious stimulus.
The patient exhibits verbatim, automatic repetition of someone else's words.
The patient automatically mimics or imitates another person's physical behavior.

threshold_count: At least 3 of 15 listed behaviors across four categories
duration_rule: 12 months (with at least 1 symptom in the past 6 months)

symptoms.description: The patient often bullies, threatens, or intimidates others.
The patient often initiates physical fights.
The patient has used a weapon that can cause serious physical harm to others (e.g., a bat, brick, broken bottle, knife, or gun).
The patient has been physically cruel to people.
The patient has been physically cruel to animals.
The patient has stolen while confronting a victim (e.g., mugging, purse snatching, extortion, or armed robbery).
The patient has forced someone into sexual activity.
The patient has deliberately engaged in fire setting with the intention of causing serious damage.
The patient has deliberately destroyed others' property by means other than fire setting.
The patient has broken into someone else's house, building, or car.
The patient often lies to obtain goods or favors or to avoid obligations (i.e., "cons" others).
The patient has stolen items of nontrivial value without confronting a victim (e.g., shoplifting without breaking and entering, or forgery).
The patient often stays out at night despite parental prohibitions, beginning before age 13 years.
The patient has run away from home overnight at least twice while living in the parental or parental surrogate home, or once without returning for a lengthy period.
The patient is often truant from school, beginning before age 13 years.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Conduct Disorder, with Limited Prosocial Emotions
threshold_count: At least 2 of 4 specific emotional features in addition to CD criteria

symptoms.description: The patient often bullies, threatens, or intimidates others.
The patient often initiates physical fights.
The patient has used a weapon that can cause serious physical harm to others (e.g., a bat, brick, broken bottle, knife, or gun).
The patient has been physically cruel to people.
The patient has been physically cruel to animals.
The patient has stolen while confronting a victim (e.g., mugging, purse snatching, extortion, or armed robbery).
The patient has forced someone into sexual activity.
The patient has deliberately engaged in fire setting with the intention of causing serious damage.
The patient has deliberately destroyed others' property by means other than fire setting.
The patient has broken into someone else's house, building, or car.
The patient often lies to obtain goods or favors or to avoid obligations (i.e., "cons" others).
The patient has stolen items of nontrivial value without confronting a victim (e.g., shoplifting without breaking and entering, or forgery).
The patient often stays out at night despite parental prohibitions, beginning before age 13 years.
The patient has run away from home overnight at least twice while living in the parental or parental surrogate home, or once without returning for a lengthy period.
The patient is often truant from school, beginning before age 13 years.
The patient lacks remorse, showing indifference to or actively rationalizing having hurt, mistreated, or stolen from another person.
The patient lacks empathy and is unwilling to recognize or identify with the feelings and needs of others.
The patient shows a lack of concern about their performance at school, work, or in other important activities.
The patient exhibits a shallow or deficient affect, demonstrating a limited expression of emotions.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Mismatch between natural body rhythms and environmental/work demands (e.g., Shift Work, Delayed Phase)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a recurring mismatch between their biological sleep-wake cycle and the environmental or social demands placed upon them.
The patient experiences notable insomnia or hypersomnolence as a result of a circadian rhythm misalignment.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Polysomnography shows 5+ central sleep apneas per hour of sleep

symptoms.description: The patient experiences frequent central sleep apneas during sleep, characterized by a cessation of breathing effort.

threshold_count: Numerous periods of hypomanic and depressive symptoms below full episode threshold
duration_rule: 2 years (1 year for children/adolescents); present at least half the time

symptoms.description: The patient has never met the full criteria for a major depressive, manic, or hypomanic episode.
The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Inability to recall important autobiographical information, usually of a stressful or traumatic nature
duration_rule: Variable; can range from minutes to many years

symptoms.description: The patient is unable to recall important autobiographical information, usually of a traumatic or stressful nature, that is too extensive to be explained by ordinary forgetfulness.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Inability to recall personal history plus sudden, purposeful travel or bewildered wandering
duration_rule: Variable; often brief (hours or days), but can be longer

symptoms.description: The patient is unable to recall important autobiographical information, usually of a traumatic or stressful nature, that is too extensive to be explained by ordinary forgetfulness.
The patient experiences apparently purposeful travel or bewildered wandering associated with amnesia for their identity or other important autobiographical information.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Motor skill performance is markedly below expectations for age and intelligence
duration_rule: Onset in the early developmental period

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits motor skill performance that is markedly below expectations for their chronological age and intelligence.
The patient experiences motor coordination difficulties that significantly interfere with academic progress or activities of daily living.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Persistent or recurrent episodes of feeling detached from one's self or surroundings while reality testing remains intact

symptoms.description: The patient experiences depersonalization, feeling detached from or as if they are an outside observer of their own mental processes or body.
The patient experiences derealization, perceiving their surroundings as unreal, dreamlike, distant, or distorted.
The patient maintains intact reality testing during episodes of depersonalization or derealization.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Marked delay in, infrequency of, or absence of ejaculation in 75%–100% of sexual activity

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a markedly delayed, infrequent, or absent orgasm despite normal sexual excitement.
The patient experiences a marked delay in or complete absence of ejaculation during partnered sexual activity.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Disturbance in attention and awareness PLUS at least one additional cognitive deficit
duration_rule: Short period (hours to a few days); symptoms typically fluctuate in severity

symptoms.description: The patient has evidence from their history, physical examination, or laboratory findings that the disturbance is a direct physiological consequence of another medical condition, substance intoxication or withdrawal, or toxin exposure.
The patient exhibits reduced awareness of the environment and difficulty directing, focusing, sustaining, or shifting attention.
The patient exhibits reduced orientation or awareness of their environment.
The patient exhibits a memory deficit, particularly difficulty learning new material or recalling recent events.
The patient experiences disorientation to time, place, or person.
The patient exhibits a language disturbance, such as rambling, disjointed speech, or difficulty finding words.
The patient exhibits a visuospatial deficit, particularly in navigating environments.
The patient exhibits a perceptual deficit, such as misinterpreting stimuli or experiencing hallucinations.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: 1 or more delusions; no other symptoms of schizophrenia

symptoms.description: The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

threshold_count: 5 or more specific traits
duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient requires an excessive amount of advice and reassurance from others to make everyday decisions.
The patient needs others to assume responsibility for most major areas of their life.
The patient has difficulty expressing disagreement with others due to an unrealistic fear of losing support or approval.
The patient has difficulty initiating projects or doing things on their own due to a lack of self-confidence rather than a lack of motivation or energy.
The patient goes to excessive lengths to obtain nurturance and support from others, to the point of volunteering to do things that are unpleasant.
The patient feels uncomfortable or helpless when alone due to exaggerated fears of being unable to care for themselves.
The patient urgently seeks another relationship as a source of care and support when a close relationship ends.
The patient is unrealistically preoccupied with fears of being left to care for themselves.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Depressive Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
threshold_count: Prominent and enduring depressed mood or loss of interest

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences a markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all, or almost all, activities most of the day.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Prominent and enduring depressed mood or loss of interest
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences a markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all, or almost all, activities most of the day.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least two clearly individual personalities, each with unique attributes
duration_rule: Not specified; typically chronic and beginning in childhood

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a disruption of identity characterized by the presence of two or more distinct personality states, each with its own pattern of perceiving and interacting with the environment.
The patient experiences recurrent memory gaps for everyday events, personal information, or traumatic events that exceed ordinary forgetfulness.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Severe tantrums 3 or more times per week; persistently irritable mood between outbursts

threshold_count: At least 2 of 6 symptoms while depressed; never without symptoms for 2+ months

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient has never been without depressive symptoms for more than 2 months at a time during the 2-year period of the disturbance.
The patient experiences poor appetite or overeating while feeling depressed.
The patient experiences insomnia or hypersomnia nearly every day.
The patient experiences fatigue or a loss of energy nearly every day.
The patient suffers from low self-esteem while experiencing depression.
The patient experiences a diminished ability to think or concentrate, or indecisiveness, nearly every day.
The patient experiences feelings of hopelessness while depressed.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Personality Change Due to Another Medical Condition
threshold_count: Enduring personality disturbance following a physical condition

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits a persistent personality disturbance that is the direct pathophysiological consequence of another medical condition.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Partnered ejaculation within approximately 1 minute of vaginal penetration and before the individual wishes

symptoms.description: The patient almost always ejaculates earlier than desired, generally within a minute of penetration.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Aroused by sex with prepubescent children; has acted on the urge OR feels severe distress/interpersonal difficulties
duration_rule: 6 months; patient must be at least 16 and at least 5 years older than the victim

symptoms.description: The patient experiences intense and recurrent sexual arousal from fantasies, urges, or behaviors involving prepubescent children.
The patient has acted upon pedophilic sexual urges involving prepubescent children.
The patient is at least 16 years of age and is at least 5 years older than the prepubescent child or children involved.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Psychological Factors Affecting Other Medical Conditions
threshold_count: 1 or more psychological or behavioral factors that adversely affect a medical condition

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits a psychological or behavioral factor that adversely affects a medical condition, interferes with treatment, or constitutes an additional health risk.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Intense longing/preoccupation plus at least 3 of 8 specified grief symptoms
duration_rule: 12 months (6 months for children); present nearly every day for the past month

symptoms.description: The patient has experienced the death of a close friend or relative.
The patient experiences intense, persistent longing or yearning for the deceased person.
The patient experiences identity disruption, such as feeling as though a part of oneself has died, since the death occurred.
The patient experiences a marked sense of disbelief regarding the death of the deceased.
The patient actively avoids reminders of the deceased person.
The patient experiences intense emotional pain, such as anger, bitter grief, or sorrow, related to the death.
The patient has difficulty moving on with life, such as experiencing problems engaging with friends, pursuing interests, or planning for the future.
The patient experiences emotional numbness, characterized by an absence or marked reduction of emotional reactivity.
The patient feels that life is meaningless or empty without the deceased person.
The patient experiences intense loneliness or feels detached from others following the death.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Habitual immediate, inordinate dread of a specific object or situation

symptoms.description: The patient avoids or endures with intense anxiety specific situations, such as flying, driving, or being in enclosed spaces.
The patient actively avoids the phobic object or situation, or endures it with intense fear or anxiety.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Persists in eating material that is not food
duration_rule: 1 month or more; patient is at least 2 years old

symptoms.description: The patient persistently eats dirt, paper, soap, or other non-nutritional, non-food substances.

threshold_count: 5 or more symptoms (at least 1 must be core mood symptom)
duration_rule: Most menstrual cycles in the past year

symptoms.description: The patient experiences marked affective lability, such as pronounced mood swings or sudden tearfulness, before menstruation.
The patient exhibits marked anger, irritability, or increased interpersonal conflicts during the premenstrual phase.
The patient experiences a markedly depressed mood, feelings of hopelessness, or self-deprecating thoughts before menstruation.
The patient experiences marked anxiety, tension, or feelings of being keyed up or on edge before menstruation.
The patient experiences a decreased interest in usual activities before menstruation.
The patient experiences subjective difficulty in concentrating during the premenstrual phase.
The patient feels lethargic, easily fatigued, or has a marked lack of energy during the premenstrual phase.
The patient experiences a significant change in appetite, overeating, or specific food cravings during the premenstrual phase.
The patient experiences hypersomnia or insomnia during the premenstrual phase.
The patient has a subjective sense of being overwhelmed or out of control during the premenstrual phase.
The patient experiences physical symptoms such as breast tenderness, muscle or joint pain, a sensation of bloating, or weight gain before menstruation.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Persistent (Chronic) Motor or Vocal Tic Disorder
threshold_count: Single or multiple motor OR vocal tics, but NOT both
duration_rule: Longer than 1 year; onset before age 18

symptoms.description: The patient experiences single or multiple motor or vocal tics, but not both, persisting for longer than one year.
The patient has a persistent history of experiencing multiple motor and vocal tics.

diagnosis_name: Psychotic Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: Obvious hallucinations or delusions
duration_rule: Not specified (physiological consequence of medical condition)

symptoms.description: The patient has a disturbance that is shown by history, physical examination, or laboratory findings to be the direct pathophysiological consequence of another medical condition.
The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: Hallucinations or delusions predominant in clinical picture
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient has symptoms that developed during or soon after substance intoxication, withdrawal, or medication exposure, with evidence that the substance is capable of producing the observed hallucinations or delusions.
The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Single or multiple motor and/or vocal tics
duration_rule: Less than 1 year; onset before age 18

symptoms.description: The patient experiences single or multiple motor and/or vocal tics that have been present for less than one year.
The patient has a persistent history of experiencing multiple motor and vocal tics.

threshold_count: 1 intrusion, 1 avoidance, 2 cognitive/mood, and 2 arousal symptoms

symptoms.description: The patient has been directly exposed to, witnessed, or learned about a traumatic event involving actual or threatened death, serious injury, or sexual violence.
The patient repeatedly relives the traumatic event through distressing memories, nightmares, or dissociative flashbacks.
The patient actively avoids memories, thoughts, feelings, or external reminders associated with the traumatic event.
The patient is unable to remember an important aspect of the traumatic event, typically due to dissociative amnesia.
The patient holds persistent and exaggerated negative beliefs or expectations about themselves, others, or the world.
The patient experiences persistent, distorted cognitions about the cause or consequences of the traumatic event, leading them to blame themselves or others.
The patient experiences a persistent negative emotional state, such as fear, horror, anger, guilt, or shame.
The patient exhibits markedly diminished interest or participation in significant activities.
The patient experiences feelings of detachment or estrangement from others.
The patient experiences a persistent inability to experience positive emotions, such as an inability to feel happiness, satisfaction, or loving feelings.
The patient exhibits irritable behavior and angry outbursts with little or no provocation, typically expressed as verbal or physical aggression toward people or objects.
The patient engages in reckless or self-destructive behavior.
The patient exhibits hypervigilance.
The patient exhibits an exaggerated startle response.
The patient experiences problems with concentration.
The patient experiences insomnia or other significant sleep disturbances.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 1 intrusion, 1 avoidance OR negative emotion, and 2 physiological symptoms
duration_rule: 1 month or more; child is under age 7

symptoms.description: The patient has been directly exposed to, witnessed, or learned about a traumatic event involving actual or threatened death, serious injury, or sexual violence.
The patient repeatedly relives the traumatic event through distressing memories, nightmares, or dissociative flashbacks.
The patient avoids or makes efforts to avoid activities, places, or physical reminders that arouse recollections of the traumatic event.
The patient avoids or makes efforts to avoid people, conversations, or interpersonal situations that arouse recollections of the traumatic event.
The patient experiences a substantially increased frequency of key negative emotional states, such as fear, sadness, guilt, or confusion.
The patient exhibits markedly diminished interest or participation in significant activities, including a constriction of play.
The patient exhibits socially withdrawn behavior.
The patient exhibits a persistent reduction in the expression of positive emotions.
The patient exhibits irritable behavior and angry outbursts with little or no provocation, typically expressed as verbal or physical aggression toward people or objects.
The patient exhibits hypervigilance.
The patient exhibits an exaggerated startle response.
The patient experiences problems with concentration.
The patient experiences insomnia or other significant sleep disturbances.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Deliberately setting multiple fires

symptoms.description: The patient deliberately and purposefully sets multiple fires.
The patient feels tension or affective arousal before setting a fire, followed by a sense of release or pleasure afterward.
The patient exhibits a fascination with or attraction to fire and its situational contexts.
The patient experiences relief or pleasure when setting fires or witnessing their aftermath.

threshold_count: Habitual failure to seek/respond to comfort plus at least 2 social/emotional disturbances
duration_rule: Symptoms present before age 5; child has developmental age of 9+ months

symptoms.description: The patient displays a consistent pattern of inhibited, emotionally withdrawn behavior toward adult caregivers, rarely seeking or responding to comfort when distressed.
The patient exhibits minimal social and emotional responsiveness to others, often accompanied by unprovoked episodes of irritability, sadness, or fearfulness.
The patient exhibits limited positive affect in their interactions with others.
The patient has experienced a pattern of extreme insufficient care, such as social neglect, frequent changes in primary caregivers, or rearing in unusual settings.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Rapid Eye Movement Sleep Behavior Disorder
threshold_count: Complex physical actions/vocalizations during REM sleep (acting out dreams)

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits vocalizations or complex motor behaviors during REM sleep, often acting out vivid dreams.
The patient lacks the normal muscle atonia that typically accompanies REM sleep, as revealed by polysomnography.

threshold_count: Irresistible urge to move legs due to unpleasant sensations, worse during rest/evenings

symptoms.description: The patient experiences an irresistible urge to move the legs, usually accompanied by uncomfortable or unpleasant sensations in the lower limbs.
The patient experiences an urge to move the legs that begins or worsens during periods of rest or inactivity.
The patient experiences an urge to move the legs that worsens in the evening or at night.
The patient experiences an urge to move that is partially or totally relieved by movement, such as walking or stretching.

threshold_count: Regurgitating and re-chewing food already eaten

symptoms.description: The patient persistently regurgitates food, which may then be re-chewed, re-swallowed, or spit out.

threshold_count: Inordinate anxiety regarding circumstances where others could closely observe the patient

symptoms.description: The patient experiences inordinate anxiety in social situations where they are exposed to possible close observation or scrutiny by others.
The patient fears acting in a way or showing anxiety symptoms that will be negatively evaluated, leading to humiliation or rejection.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Aroused by another person's suffering; fantasies/behaviors cause distress/impairment OR has acted upon urges with nonconsenting person

symptoms.description: The patient experiences sexual arousal from the physical or psychological suffering of another person.
The patient acts upon sexual sadism urges with nonconsenting persons.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Persistent difficulties in the social use of verbal and nonverbal communication
duration_rule: Onset in the early developmental period

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits deficits in the social use of verbal and nonverbal communication.
The patient has difficulty adapting their communication to fit the context or the needs of the listener.
The patient has difficulty following the rules for conversation and storytelling, such as taking turns or rephrasing when misunderstood.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: 2 or more (at least one must be delusions, hallucinations, or disorganized speech)
duration_rule: 6 months continuous evidence (1 month active-phase symptoms)

symptoms.description: The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient exhibits speech that is noticeably incoherent, disorganized, or frequently derails from the topic.
The patient exhibits severely abnormal psychomotor behavior, ranging from unresponsiveness to agitated, purposeless movement.
The patient displays negative symptoms, such as diminished emotional expression, restricted affect, or a lack of volition.
The patient experiences clinically significant impairment in occupational, academic, or social functioning due to their symptoms.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

threshold_count: 4 or more specific traits
duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient demonstrates a pervasive pattern of detachment from social relationships and a restricted range of emotional expression.
The patient strongly prefers solitary activities and neither desires nor enjoys close relationships, including being part of a family.
The patient has little, if any, interest in having sexual experiences with another person.
The patient takes pleasure in few, if any, activities.
The patient lacks close friends or confidants other than first-degree relatives.
The patient appears indifferent to the praise or criticism of others.
The patient appears emotionally cold, detached, or demonstrates a flattened affect, seeming indifferent to the praise or criticism of others.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 5 or more specific traits
duration_rule: Enduring pattern present from early adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient experiences ideas of reference, interpreting casual incidents and external events as having a particular and unusual meaning specifically for them, excluding actual delusions of reference.
The patient exhibits odd beliefs or magical thinking that influences their behavior and is inconsistent with subcultural norms.
The patient experiences unusual perceptual experiences, including bodily illusions.
The patient displays odd thinking and speech, such as being vague, metaphorical, overelaborate, or stereotyped.
The patient exhibits suspiciousness or paranoid ideation.
The patient exhibits an inappropriate or constricted affect in social and interpersonal situations.
The patient exhibits behavior or an appearance that is odd, eccentric, or peculiar.
The patient lacks close friends or confidants other than first-degree relatives.
The patient experiences excessive social anxiety that does not diminish with familiarity and tends to be associated with paranoid fears rather than negative judgments about self.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: Principal symptoms of schizophrenia coexist with a major mood episode; delusions/hallucinations for 2 weeks in absence of mood episode

symptoms.description: The patient has experienced delusions or hallucinations for two or more weeks in the absence of a major mood episode during the lifetime duration of the illness.
The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

chapter_category: Schizophrenia Spectrum and Other Psychotic Disorders
threshold_count: At least 2 (at least one must be delusions, hallucinations, or disorganized speech)

symptoms.description: The patient holds firmly fixed false beliefs that persist despite contradictory evidence.
The patient experiences false sensory perceptions, most commonly auditory or visual, without any external stimulus.
The patient exhibits speech that is noticeably incoherent, disorganized, or frequently derails from the topic.
The patient exhibits severely abnormal psychomotor behavior, ranging from unresponsiveness to agitated, purposeless movement.
The patient displays negative symptoms, such as diminished emotional expression, restricted affect, or a lack of volition.
The patient exhibits symptoms that are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.

threshold_count: Consistent failure to speak in specific social situations despite speaking normally at other times
duration_rule: 1 month (excluding the first month of school)

symptoms.description: The patient consistently fails to speak in specific social situations where there is an expectation for speaking, despite speaking normally in other situations.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 3 or more symptoms concerning separation from attachment figures
duration_rule: 6 months for adults; 4 weeks for children

symptoms.description: The patient experiences severe anxiety or distress when anticipating or experiencing separation from home or major attachment figures.
The patient excessively worries about losing major attachment figures or about possible harm coming to them, such as illness, injury, disasters, or death.
The patient excessively worries about experiencing an untoward event, such as getting lost or being kidnapped, that would cause separation from a major attachment figure.
The patient is persistently reluctant or refuses to go out, attend school, or sleep away from home due to fear of separation.
The patient experiences excessive fear or reluctance about being alone or without major attachment figures.
The patient exhibits reluctance or refusal to sleep away from home or to go to sleep without being near a major attachment figure.
The patient has repeated nightmares involving themes of separation.
The patient repeatedly complains of physical symptoms, such as headaches or nausea, when separated from major attachment figures.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Prominent and severe sexual dysfunction clinically attributable to a substance
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient experiences clinically significant sexual dysfunction that appears directly caused by substance intoxication, substance withdrawal, or medication use.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least 1 symptom in reading, math, or written expression markedly below expected level
duration_rule: Persistent for at least 6 months despite intervention

symptoms.description: The patient has academic skills that are markedly below the expected level for their chronological age.
The patient scores significantly below norms on standardized tests in reading, mathematics, or writing.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Repetitive, seemingly driven, and purposeless motor behavior
duration_rule: Onset in the early developmental period

symptoms.description: The patient engages in repetitive, abnormally frequent, non-goal-directed movements.
The patient exhibits motor behavior that significantly interferes with their social or academic activities.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Persistent difficulty with speech sound production that interferes with speech intelligibility

symptoms.description: The patient experiences persistent difficulty with speech sound production.
The patient has limited speech intelligibility that significantly interferes with effective communication.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Polysomnography shows periods of reduced breathing with elevated CO2 levels

symptoms.description: The patient experiences episodes of shallow breathing or hypoventilation during sleep that lead to abnormal blood gas levels.
The patient demonstrates periods of reduced breathing accompanied by elevated carbon dioxide levels during polysomnography.

threshold_count: 1 or more distressing or disruptive somatic symptoms plus 1 or more health-related thoughts/behaviors

symptoms.description: The patient experiences one or more distressing physical symptoms that significantly disrupt their daily life.
The patient invests excessive time and energy in health care or experiences persistent, high-level anxiety about the seriousness of their symptoms.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Somatic Symptom Disorder, with Predominant Pain
threshold_count: 1 or more distressing somatic symptoms (primarily pain) plus 1 or more health-related thoughts/behaviors

symptoms.description: The patient experiences severe, often chronic physical pain as their predominant somatic complaint, which lacks an apparent physical cause or exceeds typical expectations.
The patient invests excessive time and energy in health care or experiences persistent, high-level anxiety about the seriousness of their symptoms.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Frequent disruptions in the normal fluency and time patterning of speech

symptoms.description: The patient experiences frequent disruptions in the normal fluency and time patterning of their speech.
The patient exhibits marked physical tension while speaking or produces words with excess tension.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Prominent and severe sleep disturbance caused by a substance
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient complains of a severe sleep disturbance, such as insomnia or hypersomnolence, directly resulting from substance or medication use.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 2 or more of 11 symptoms

symptoms.description: The patient frequently consumes the substance in larger amounts or over a longer period than originally intended.
The patient expresses a persistent desire or makes unsuccessful efforts to cut down or control their substance use.
The patient spends a great deal of time in activities necessary to obtain the substance, use it, or recover from its effects.
The patient experiences an intense craving or a strong urge to use the specific substance.
The patient engages in recurrent substance use that results in a failure to fulfill major role obligations at work, school, or home.
The patient experiences significant interpersonal problems, withdrawal from social or recreational activities, or failure to fulfill major role obligations due to substance use.
The patient gives up or significantly reduces important social, occupational, or recreational activities due to substance use.
The patient repeatedly uses the substance in physically hazardous situations or continues use despite knowing it causes ongoing physical or psychological harm.
The patient continues to use the substance despite knowing it causes or exacerbates a persistent physical or psychological problem.
The patient develops tolerance, requiring markedly increased amounts of the substance to achieve the desired effect, or experiencing diminished effects with the same amount.
The patient experiences characteristic withdrawal symptoms when the substance is discontinued, or takes the substance to relieve or avoid withdrawal symptoms.

threshold_count: Multiple motor AND 1 or more vocal tics
duration_rule: Longer than 1 year; onset before age 18

symptoms.description: The patient experiences rapid, recurrent, nonrhythmic motor movements, such as eye blinking or facial grimacing.
The patient exhibits abrupt, recurrent vocalizations, such as grunting, throat clearing, or uttering specific words.
The patient has a persistent history of experiencing multiple motor and vocal tics.

threshold_count: Arousal by cross-dressing (thoughts, behaviors, or urges) has repeatedly caused distress or impairment

symptoms.description: The patient experiences recurrent, intense sexual arousal from the act of cross-dressing.
The patient experiences clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to cross-dressing fantasies, urges, or behaviors.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Repeated hair pulling resulting in hair loss plus repeated attempts to stop

symptoms.description: The patient repeatedly pulls out their own hair, resulting in noticeable hair loss.
The patient makes repeated, unsuccessful attempts to decrease or stop their hair-pulling behavior.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Anxiety symptoms present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient presents with anxiety symptoms, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Symptoms characteristic of communication disorder but lacking specific information

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits symptoms characteristic of a communication disorder that cause impairment, but there is insufficient information to specify the exact diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Unspecified Disruptive, Impulse-Control, and Conduct Disorder
threshold_count: Disruptive behavior present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits disruptive behavior, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Dissociative symptoms present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits dissociative symptoms, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Eating/feeding symptoms present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits eating or feeding symptoms, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Elimination symptoms present but insufficient information for specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits symptoms of an elimination disorder, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Gender dysphoria symptoms present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient presents with symptoms of gender dysphoria, but there is insufficient information to make a definitive diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Cannot be reliably assessed due to physical or mental impairment
duration_rule: Child is at least 5 years old

symptoms.description: The patient has intellectual functioning that cannot be reliably assessed by standardized testing due to sensory, physical, or severe behavioral impairments.

threshold_count: Mood symptoms present but insufficient information for specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient presents with mood symptoms, but there is insufficient information for a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Obsessive-compulsive symptoms present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient presents with obsessive-compulsive symptoms, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Paraphilic disturbance present but clinician decide not to specify the reason

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits a paraphilic disturbance, but the clinician chooses not to specify the reason the criteria are not met for a specific paraphilic disorder.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Sexual problem present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient has sexual dysfunction symptoms causing clinically significant distress, but the specific diagnostic criteria are unmet and the clinician chooses not to specify the reason.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Unspecified Somatic Symptom and Related Disorder
threshold_count: Somatic symptoms present but insufficient information for a specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient presents with somatic symptoms, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Trauma/stressor symptoms present but insufficient information for specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient presents with trauma-related symptoms causing distress or impairment, but there is insufficient information to make a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Aroused by watching an unwary person disrobing or having sex; has repeatedly acted on the urge OR feels distress/disability
duration_rule: 6 months; patient must be at least 18 years old

symptoms.description: The patient experiences recurrent, intense sexual arousal from observing an unsuspecting person who is naked, in the process of disrobing, or engaging in sexual activity.
The patient has acted upon voyeuristic sexual urges with a nonconsenting person.
The patient is at least 18 years of age.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
